perturbation.original_text: During the Term, without the Company's written consent, Principal shall not, either in his individual capacity or as an agent for another: (i) hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents; (ii) entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; (iii) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; or (iv) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term, without the Company's written consent, Principal shall not, either in his individual capacity or as an agent for another: (i) hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents, unless the officer is underperforming; (ii) entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; (iii) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; or (iv) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.explanation: The added condition, "unless the officer is underperforming," creates a contradiction. The original clause strictly prohibits Principal from hiring or offering employment to the specified entities' officers, employees or agents. The modified clause introduces an exception based on performance, creating an ambiguous situation as the criteria for 'underperforming' is not defined, introducing ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, without the Company's written consent, Principal shall not, either in his individual capacity or as an agent for another: (i) hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents, unless the officer is underperforming; (ii) entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; (iii) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; or (iv) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the condition 'unless the officer is underperforming' to the original clause prohibiting Principal from hiring officers, employees or agents. This creates a direct conflict with the intention of the original clause, as it introduces an exception based on a subjective criterion. This new exception introduces ambiguity in enforcement, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the criteria for 'underperforming' is not defined. As the explanation states, 'The modified clause introduces an exception based on performance, creating an ambiguous situation as the criteria for 'underperforming' is not defined, introducing ambiguity in enforcement.' This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction and results in a clear and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Phoenix Footwear, PGG Acquisition and Principal acknowledge and recognize that these covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, that without the protection of such covenants, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would not have entered into the Merger Agreement, that the consideration paid by Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition under the Merger Agreement bears no relationship to the damages Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition may suffer in the event of any breach of the covenants, and that such covenants contain reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained necessary to protect Phoenix Footwear's and PFG Acquisition's business interests. If this Section 2 shall for any reason be held excessively broad as to time, duration, geographical scope, activity or subject, it shall be enforceable to the extent compatible with then-applicable laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Phoenix Footwear, PGG Acquisition and Principal acknowledge and recognize that these covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, that without the protection of such covenants, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would not have entered into the Merger Agreement, that the consideration paid by Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition under the Merger Agreement bears no relationship to the damages Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition may suffer in the event of any breach of the covenants, and that such covenants contain reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained necessary to protect Phoenix Footwear's and PFG Acquisition's business interests. Principal is free to ignore anything in Section 2, and is voidable for breach.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the importance and reasonableness of the non-compete covenants. The added clause, "Principal is free to ignore anything in Section 2, and is voidable for breach" contradicts the previous statements, explicitly allowing Principal to disregard the covenants and voiding the entire section. This creates a direct in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Phoenix Footwear, PGG Acquisition and Principal acknowledge and recognize that these covenants not to compete are integral to the Merger Agreement, that without the protection of such covenants, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition would not have entered into the Merger Agreement, that the consideration paid by Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition under the Merger Agreement bears no relationship to the damages Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition may suffer in the event of any breach of the covenants, and that such covenants contain reasonable limitations as to time, geographical area and scope of activity to be restrained necessary to protect Phoenix Footwear's and PFG Acquisition's business interests. Principal is free to ignore anything in Section 2, and is voidable for breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'Principal is free to ignore anything in Section 2, and is voidable for breach' to the original text. This directly contradicts the initial acknowledgment that the non-compete covenants are integral to the Merger Agreement and necessary for Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition to have entered the agreement. By explicitly allowing Principal to ignore Section 2, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction with the acknowledged importance and enforceability of the non-compete covenants, making the contract unclear and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case, because the added phrase contradicts the original, unchanged portion.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans. The parties hereto agree that the failure of any Confidential Information to be marked or otherwise labeled as confidential or proprietary information shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans. However, any of this information disclosed before signing the agreement is no longer confidential. The parties hereto agree that the failure of any Confidential Information to be marked or otherwise labeled as confidential or proprietary information shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" broadly. The addition of "However, any of this information disclosed before signing the agreement is no longer confidential" contradicts this broad definition. The original text states that Confidential Information includes, without limitation, the listed items, regardless of whether they are marked confidential. However, because it says that any of this information disclosed before the agreement is signed is no longer confidential, it contradicts the Confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans. However, any of this information disclosed before signing the agreement is no longer confidential. The parties hereto agree that the failure of any Confidential Information to be marked or otherwise labeled as confidential or proprietary information shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the clause 'However, any of this information disclosed before signing the agreement is no longer confidential.' This directly contradicts the original definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,' which includes a broad range of materials 'whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright' and states that the failure to mark it as confidential does not affect its status. The added clause introduces an exception based on timing of disclosure, creating a contradiction as the agreement no longer protects previously disclosed information that would otherwise be considered CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION under the original definition.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Principal may use, exploit, or disclose Confidential Information, but only to the extent that such Confidential Information (i) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Principal; or (ii) is disclosed pursuant to the requirement of a governmental agency or a court of law or otherwise required by operation of law, provided that Principal gives PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear prompt written notice of such requirement prior to disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Principal may use, exploit, or disclose Confidential Information without restriction, unless PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear can prove it to be a wrongful act in a court of law; or (ii) is disclosed pursuant to the requirement of a governmental agency or a court of law or otherwise required by operation of law, provided that Principal gives PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear prompt written notice of such requirement prior to disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause outlines specific, limited circumstances under which Principal may disclose Confidential Information. The modified clause, "Principal may use, exploit, or disclose Confidential Information without restriction, unless PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear can prove it to be a wrongful act in a court of law," significantly broadens the permitted use and disclosure, shifting the burden of proof to PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear and contradicting the limited exceptions of the original provision. This means principal can disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION unless there is sufficient proof of a wrongful act by the company, that contradicts the limited actions Principal could take.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Principal may use, exploit, or disclose Confidential Information without restriction, unless PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear can prove it to be a wrongful act in a court of law; or (ii) is disclosed pursuant to the requirement of a governmental agency or a court of law or otherwise required by operation of law, provided that Principal gives PFG Acquisition and Phoenix Footwear prompt written notice of such requirement prior to disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text outlines specific conditions for disclosing confidential information. The changed text broadens this, allowing disclosure without restriction unless proven wrongful in court. This directly contradicts the implicit restrictions set in the original text and reiterated in the provided contradicted text, thus fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, because the clause appears with conflicting implications. The explanation states 'This means principal can disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION unless there is sufficient proof of a wrongful act by the company, that contradicts the limited actions Principal could take.'
